heroIntro: Pyrography artist from Germany. I burn stories into wood — line by line, spark by spark.
storyHeading: From the first spark to the final stroke.
storyParagraphs: I held my first burning iron at twelve — borrowed from my grandfather, originally meant for model making. “Just a quick try” turned into many hours. And the hours turned into a craft.
Today I work in a small workshop at the edge of the woods. Birch, oak, sometimes lime. A sketch on tracing paper, then contours, then shadow layer by layer. Nothing is painted — everything is burned.
I'm fascinated by transitions: the moment embers turn to ash, an animal becomes a myth, a line becomes a story. That's what every piece is about.
processIntro: Four phases, many hours — every piece goes through them in this order.

processSteps.title: Choosing the motif
processSteps.text: It always starts with the motif — working out how it will translate onto the wood and how to realise it.

processSteps.title: Tracing onto the wood
processSteps.text: The wood is selected and sanded, and the motif is traced onto it.

processSteps.title: Burning
processSteps.text:
Contours first, then deeper and deeper.
Several layers are needed to bring out depth through the line work.

processSteps.title: Finish
processSteps.text: Cleaning up the burn marks, sealing with natural oil.

materialText: Every type of wood has its own character that I want to discover and get to know.
toolsText: A Brennpeter as my base tool. I'm still learning which tips work best for what — so far I've focused mostly on the precision iron.

timeline.title: The first spark
timeline.subtitle: I discovered this art form by chance during a rehab stay. I was fascinated from the very beginning — and it hasn't let go of me since.

ctaText: Commissions and questions — feel free to use the contact form.
